page: Amazon Web Services r5ad.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz and includes 8 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Storage is provided via a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, and network performance is rated at a 2 Gbps baseline.

Benchmark results indicate that the r5ad.2xlarge exhibits modest compute capabilities. In stress-ng testing, single-core performance scored 591.09 and multi-core performance reached 2361.55. Memory bandwidth testing showed a read-only small block speed of 310.3 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 13.03 GB/sec. Passmark testing yielded average results, with a CPU score of 6900.58 and a memory score of 1875.47. Database operations under Passmark scored 1950.73, while Redis SET operations achieved 4.98 million operations per second. For lightweight machine learning, the instance achieved 92.02 tokens per second for text generation using a small 135M model, but performance dropped to 4.88 tokens per second on a 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5ad.2xlarge offers a high density of DDR4 memory and local NVMe SSD storage relative to its compute tier, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-frequency CPU performance. However, the trade-off is lower single-threaded and multi-threaded processing speeds. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers, database operations, and workloads requiring fast local temporary storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 2.2 GHz clock speed. It is configured with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, running on the Nitro hypervisor. Network performance is supported by a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates modest compute capabilities, with a Passmark CPU score of 6900.58 and a Geekbench single-core score of 727.0. Memory bandwidth reaches 310.3 GB/sec for small cached blocks, while Redis SET operations perform at 4.98 million operations per second. This instance offers a high memory-to-core ratio, making it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound applications. It is best suited for database operations, caching layers, and workloads requiring local temporary storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 300 GB NVMe SSD local instance store, designed for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high-capacity DDR4 memory, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
Includes a 300 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume
Features a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and local storage workloads
